issues.comment: Test cleanup uses the literal alias "alias" instead of the actual alias of the created IDP (e.g., "idp-alias-0" through "idp-alias-19"). The cleanup code `getCleanup().addCleanup(testRealm().identityProviders().get("alias")::remove)` should use the actual alias like `"idp-alias-" + i`. This means cleanup will attempt to remove a non-existent IDP named "alias", while the 20+ IDPs created during the test persist and pollute subsequent tests. Same bug exists at line 422 for "idp-alias-20". This is a copy-paste error from the pre-existing `testCacheIDPByOrg` test (line 302) which has the same bug.

Test cleanup registers removal of IDP with literal alias "alias" instead of the actual alias "idp-alias-20". Should be `getCleanup().addCleanup(testRealm().identityProviders().get("idp-alias-20")::remove)`. The created IDP will not be cleaned up, leaking into subsequent tests.

The new predicate `.and(idp -> idp.getOrganizationId() == null || Boolean.parseBoolean(idp.getConfig().get(OrganizationModel.BROKER_PUBLIC)))` added to `getLoginPredicate()` is too restrictive. It causes `registerIDPLoginInvalidation()` to skip cache invalidation for org-linked IDPs that don't have BROKER_PUBLIC set. However, `getForLogin(ORG_ONLY, orgId)` returns all org-linked IDPs matching login search options without filtering by BROKER_PUBLIC, so those IDPs can be cached. When such an IDP is removed, the login cache won't be invalidated because the predicate returns false. This predicate/delegate mismatch is a design inconsistency that could cause subtle cache staleness, especially in `registerIDPLoginInvalidationOnUpdate` where there's no self-healing fallback.
